title: Introduction to Databases
summary: Understand what databases are, why we use them, and the difference between relational and non-relational databases.
content_html:
A database is an organized collection of structured data, managed by a Database Management System (DBMS).

Why Use Databases?

  • Eliminate data redundancy and inconsistency
  • Enable concurrent multi-user access
  • Enforce data integrity and security
  • Support efficient querying via SQL

Relational vs Non-Relational

Relational (SQL)Non-Relational (NoSQL)
Tables with rows/columnsDocuments, key-value, graph
Strict schemaFlexible schema
MySQL, PostgreSQL, OracleMongoDB, Redis, Neo4j

Key Terminology

  • Table (Relation): grid of rows and columns
  • Row (Tuple/Record): one data entry
  • Column (Attribute): one data field
  • Primary Key: unique identifier for each row
  • Foreign Key: column linking to another table's primary key

exercises.title: Primary key
exercises.prompt: A primary key in a relational table must be:
options: Unique for each row and non-null
exercises.answer: Unique for each row and non-null

exercises.title: Foreign key
options: References a primary key in another table
Is the main identifier of a row
exercises.answer: References a primary key in another table

exercises.answer: Slower data access for small datasets
